programmes.show_description: The team help the survivor of a horrific house fire who wants to cover his scarring with a Celtic knot design, and a woman who wears make-up to cover her shocking neck tattoo.
Greg Davies' assistant Alex Horne is powerless in the face of Ed Gamble's creative vision. Plus: bare chests and bucking donkeys.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jason Manford and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Fay Ripley. Alex Horne and The Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Warren Boone, the chief people officer of restaurant chain Coco's Bakery, steps off the corporate ladder to get a taste of life on the company's front line.
Mayor of Fontana, Acquanetta Warren, goes undercover in the Californian city to spend time with sewer workers and the fire brigade to see how the city is recovering from Covid.
In Bournemouth, there are hard-to-please guests, intriguing photos and interesting impressions, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
In South Wales, Rachael and Nick cook dishes they've never made before, Ameel shows off his powers of memory recall, and Angela bosses Jeff around as they prepare their no-nonsense menu.
...Selling: Nyahsa and Hezekiyah's hopes of fixing up their home quickly turned into a nightmare. Can Jonathan and Drew dream up an innovative solution?
The One with All the Pranks: Perfectionist Maria places her dream of a country house wedding in the hands of her fiancé Sam. But Sam loves a good prank. Could this be a problem?
The Drive-in One: Mummy's boy Tiago plans to put his self-conscious bride Karlette on the big screen at his 1950s drive-in wedding, but she wants something intimate and contemporary.
Paul Damico, president of Moe's Southwest Grill, one of the world's fastest-growing restaurant chains, goes undercover to find out how efficient his organisation is.
Sara Bittorf, the chief brand officer of Boston Market - whose 470 restaurants serve home-style comfort food - goes undercover to work alongside the chain's employees.
Roosevelt Inn: Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the owner of the Roosevelt Inn in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho is busy cracking jokes and playing dress up. Can Gordon help?
Cambridge Hotel: Gordon visits Cambridge, NY, where ex-military man John Imhof runs the Cambridge Hotel like a dictatorship, alienating his staff and guests.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Andrea and Santino's dream of moving back downtown is a waking nightmare until the brothers step in with massive renovations.
Tender Bender: Gary crashes the tender. Danni and Keith continue to flirt, but a cute host catches Danni's eye. And Gary and Daisy's friendship is still rocky.
Our five comics devise new ways to help Alex Horne make the Taskmaster's breakfast. Plus: former series champion Kerry Godliman makes a dramatic return.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. Dr John Cooper Clarke's in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Alan Carr and Josh Widdicombe take on Joe Wilkinson and Sara Pascoe. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. With Rachel Riley.
The Other Jackie: The award-winning comedy about the Goodman family. Mum and Dad get a hot tub, and Jim brings a date round for a disastrous dinner.
The Tin of Meat: Mum's friend Val moves in and causes havoc when she finds out that Dad is hiding a tin of 20-year-old meat in his shed.
The team step in to help Georgie, who had such a wild time with the boys on holiday she commemorated the trip with a tattoo that's left her single ever since.
Our five comics devise new ways to help Alex Horne make the Taskmaster's breakfast. Plus: former series champion Kerry Godliman makes a dramatic return.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Kevin Bridges and Jessica Knappett. Dr John Cooper Clarke's in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Linda L. Chadwick, CEO of Rita's Italian Ice & Frozen Custard, goes undercover to find out how her new initiatives are working and to help franchisees become more profitable.
Andrew Simon, CEO of Fremont Street Experience, who started at the Las Vegas attraction at the start of the pandemic, goes undercover to discover and address issues.
In Durham, Steph and James serve gastropub grub, but can they avoid falling out? Gary and Amanda's pig joins their party. And on the final night, the guests discover Andy's reward chart!
In Blackpool, ex-dancer Rowan and businessman John take on hard-to-please Holly and Ollie, and Sally and Joanne, who wear matching tie-dye, in a week featuring a belly-busting breakfast.
...Selling: It's convention vs contemporary when Krystal and Cam can't agree on a dream home. While Drew looks for a compromise, Jonathan works on costly constructions.
The One with the Rally Cars: Adrenaline junkie Mark wants to fly high and take to the sky on his big day, but his straight-talking bride Lynsey wants more personal, no-nonsense nuptials.
The One at a Welsh Theme Park: Thrill-seeking Tom wants to give the love of his life Becca the ride of her life, as he plans their wedding at his favourite theme park.
CEO Andy Wirth takes on a new identity to work incognito among his own employees at a gigantic ski resort near Lake Tahoe.
Fatburger, based in Los Angeles, is known as the 'last great hamburger stand'. CEO Andy Wiederhorn dons a disguise to go undercover in his own organisation.
Juniper Hill Inn (Part 1/2): Gordon visits the historic Juniper Hill Inn in Vermont, which looks like a million dollars but smells of sewage...
Juniper Hill Inn (Part 2/2): The issues keep coming at the Juniper Hill Inn in Vermont. Can Gordon help the staff and owners to communicate? Or will he be forced to abandon ship?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Fire chief Joe and his two daughters are stoked to move out of their bland home. But can he find a new place that sparks his girls' interest?
Let the Bottles Hit the Floor: Splitting the bill drives a wedge between Cloyce and the crew, while a severe sail causes liquor bottles to come crashing down.
Jo Brand demonstrates an impressive grasp of street slang and David Baddiel takes a sledgehammer approach. Former series champion Katherine Ryan returns.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Lee Mack and Victoria Coren Mitchell take on Alan Carr and Dane Baptiste. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Johnny Vegas and Rhod Gilbert take on Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Surprise: Mum plans her own surprise birthday party, which really backfires when Dad gets involved. Get ready for the party from hell.
Lord Luck: Dad annoys everyone with his old ventriloquist dummy Lord Luck, while Jonny's recently dumped friend Ben comes for dinner in need of cheering up.
